arts.ttl: Guardiola praises Haaland’s self-control as City prepare for next Arsenal face-off
arts.sum: Manager believes defenders treat Haaland roughly ‘He never complains. He behaves incredibly well’ Pep Guardiola has lauded Erling Haaland for showing self-control despite his rough treatment from Premier League defenders doing whatever they can to stop the Manchester City striker. One of the few times Haaland’s discipline has lapsed was in the reverse fixture against Sunday’s opponents, Arsenal, earlier this season, when he was involved in a match-long physical battle with the centre-backs Gabriel Magalhães and William Saliba. When John Stones scored a 97th-minute equaliser for City, Haaland threw the ball at the back of the Brazilian’s head while celebrating. That sparked a heated confrontation before the striker told the Arsenal manager, Mikel Arteta, to “stay humble” after the final whistle. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola hints under-fire Man City man could get permanent position switch
arts.sum: Manchester City have a right-back conundrum after sanctioning one deal in January and seemingly deciding against another.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola calls for Erling Haaland to receive more protection from referees as Man City striker prepares to renew his rivalry with Gabriel
arts.sum: JOE BERNSTEIN: The Manchester City striker renews his rivalry with Arsenal centre back Gabriel for the first time on Sunday since the Norwegian threw a ball at his opponent's head.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta sends Arsenal squad warning over two heated Erling Haaland incidents
arts.sum: Manchester City striker told Mikel Arteta to "stay humble" after the Premier League champions snatched a late point against 10-man Arsenal at the Etihad Stadium back in September

arts.ttl: Kieran Tierney alternative hands Celtic major transfer boost as Luis Palma breaks silence on loan exit
arts.sum: Manchester United full back Tyrell Malacia - who has been touted as a potential Hoops target - has reportedly seen a move to Benfica fall through

arts.ttl: Man Utd announce Arsenal raid as Chelsea seal three transfers: FT recap
arts.sum: Plus, Chelsea reached a total agreement for three deals and Aston Villa closed in on Man Utd outcast Marcus Rashford . Aston Villa are close to reaching an agreement to sign Man Utd outcast Marcus Rashford on loan.

arts.ttl: Forgotten Arsenal talent shows Gunners what they're missing as stunning goal run continues
arts.sum: Arsenal want to sign a forward in the transfer window but have so far been unable to do so and a former striker is in fine form having recently moved to Monaco to continue his career

arts.ttl: Real Madrid suffer major Champions League blow with Manchester City on the horizon
arts.sum: Real Madrid haven’t had their injury problems to seek in recent times and Carlo Ancelotti has now been dealt another huge potential blow just 10 days before the mouthwatering Champions League knockout playoff first leg against Manchester City. In Saturday’s LaLiga game away to Espanyol, central defender Antonio Rüdiger was forced off the field after only 15 minutes, with youngster Raúl Asencio taking his place.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough in talks to sign Aston Villa winger as Michael Carrick's side also look to beat Celtic to former Manchester City star
arts.sum: SIMON JONES: Middlesbrough are in talks to sign a winger from Aston Villa. Michael Carrick's side are also looking to beat Celtic to a former Manchester City star.

arts.ttl: Liverpool overcome big Bournemouth hurdle to put spotlight on Arsenal
arts.sum: Where Arsenal, Nottingham Forest and Manchester City failed before them, a Mohamed Salah-inspired Liverpool prevailed, winning at AFC Bournemouth as Arne Slot's Reds went nine points clear at the top of the Premier League. Andoni Iraola's side had beaten the second, third and fourth-placed teams at home this season and were looking to complete the set on Saturday and dent their opponents' hopes of winning only a second-ever Premier League title.
